question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of the reported funding cut to Adapt NI from April 2026, to detail any assessment he has made of the impact this reduction will have on adults with hearing loss, and deafness, particularly those who rely on Adapt NI.
answer_text: The Local Growth Fund in Northern Ireland is administered directly by the UK Government.   When the fund allocations for 2026/27 are finalised, my department will assess the potential impact on achieving departmental objectives.	My department continues to work with Adapt NI as a valued delivery partner of the Workable (NI) and Access to Work (NI) employment programmes, which provide support and assessments for adults with hearing loss and deafness in work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current waiting times for Personal Independence Payment appeals.
answer_text: The Appeals Service (NI) does not specifically track waiting times for Personal Independence Payment Appeals, due to the varying complexity of each individual case. However, key process timeframes would indicate the following:Financial YearAverage Timeframe for Appeal Received to First Date of Hearing (weeks)Average Timeframe for Appeal Received to Date Disposed (Appeal Closed)(weeks)2025/26*41.2847.61*Year To Date (01 April 2025 to 15 February 2026)

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any support available, through his Department, for the provision of breakfast clubs in schools.
answer_text: In the 2025/26 financial year, over £8 million has been allocated to schools through the Extended Schools programme, which aims to improve educational outcomes for disadvantaged children and young people by providing the additional support they may need to reach their full potential. This programme represents the largest source of Department of Educationfunded breakfast club provision in Northern Ireland.In 2024/25, a total of 167 breakfast clubs were delivered through Extended Schools and Extended Schools clusters, providing valuable support to children and families in participating communities. Funding used for breakfast clubs cannot be disaggregated from wider funding allocations made under the Extended Schools programme.Schools in Northern Ireland that are not eligible for the Extended Schools programme may also provide breakfast clubs using other sources of funding or through third-party partnerships.  A record of such provision is not collated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) whether he has any plans to offer zero, or low-interest loans for homeowners to install solar panels; and (ii) whether there will be a dedicated grant scheme for the installation of solar panels for homeowners.
answer_text: The Windsor Framework enables the extension to the North of the VAT relief for energy saving materials that was previously available only in Britain. This means that zero rates of VAT currently apply on installation of energy-saving materials such as solar panels.Feedback from the low carbon heat consultation in 2025 suggested that combining solar panels with a heat pump could reduce overall operating costs. This option is being considered as we take forward the development of the low carbon heat and energy efficiency support scheme in 2026. In the meantime, households may still find solar panels or solar with battery storage, a worthwhile investment offering long-term savings, even in the absence of grant support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any consultations her Department is undertaking in relation to the student loan repayment system; and (ii) whether she has any plans to introduce reforms to that system.
answer_text: All borrowers from here are subject to “Plan 1” terms and conditions. This plan type also includes borrowers from England, Scotland and Wales, therefore the terms and conditions of this plan type are not a devolved matter and cannot be unilaterally changed.Engagement is continuing on the development of draft Terms of Reference for a wider Review of Higher Education Funding. It is proposed that the review will include consideration of tuition fee loan repayment plans. The overall timeframe for the review will be determined once the Terms of Reference are finalised and subject to the availability of resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current waiting times for occupational therapy referrals, including the average time from referral to first appointment, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Please see the table below which details the number of people currently recorded on Occupational Therapy waiting lists.  The Health and Social Care Trusts do not collect data regarding average waiting times therefore this information cannot be provided.The WHSCT advise that since the implementation of the new Encompass regional framework on the 8th May 2025, the Trust continues to undertake extensive data validation with service teams and the data supplied in this response may be subject to change.  All 5 Health and Social Care Trusts highlight that waiting lists are subject to manual validation and adjustments due to workflow issues.HSC TRUSTBHSCTNHSCTSEHSCTSHSCTWHSCTTotal Waiting3,0016,7783,0253,9374,844Waiting greater than 13 Weeks1,3264,7161,8152,9963,760

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any assessment his Department has made of leisure and fitness provision in Whitehead, particularly in light of an ageing population, and the role preventative health and physical activity has in reducing falls, frailty and pressure on health and social care services.
answer_text: Under Article 10 of the Recreation and Youth Service (Northern Ireland) Order 1986, district councils have a statutory responsibility to ensure the provision of adequate recreational, social, physical and cultural facilities within their areas which includes the establishment and maintenance of leisure and sport facilities. Sport NI, an Arms Length Body of my Department, have advised me that over the past five years, Sport NI has invested £39,516 at four sports clubs in Whitehead to sustain or improve sports facility provision.‘Active Living’ the Sport and Physical Activity Strategy for Northern Ireland recognises the positive impact that participation in sport and physical activity has on both physical and mental health across all age groups, including older people.However, the issues raised in your question regarding preventative health, including the role of physical activity in reducing falls, frailty and the associated pressures on health and social care services would fall within the remit of the Department of Health, which holds policy responsibility for fall prevention, healthy ageing and adult health and social care.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 37818/22-27, to detail the number of  health and social care staff  who have completed the new autism and neurodiversity awareness training since its launch, broken down by (i) Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) staff group or job role including, but not limited to, medical, nursing, allied health professionals, social work, and administrative staff, as of 1 January 2026.
